domain: legal-citation retrieval (CourtListener), cosine-ranking consumer, LaBSE embeddings -- real large corpus, non-physical consumer
class: CONFIRMED (rehabilitation of the GO-P-2026-035 honest negative)
fix: the GO-P-2026-035 miss used an ESTIMATED read operator (centered doc covariance) that overfit calibration and did not transfer. Replaced with the GO-1 BLIND-PROBE read operator: S = mean_i g_i g_i^T with g_i = a_hat - cos(a_i,b_i) b_hat (the ranking margin sensitivity, recovered from the consumer). This strips the anisotropy (in the subtracted cos*b_hat term), is task-tied, kept LOW-RANK (top-r), and the config was selected on an INTERNAL cal-A/cal-B split (fit on cal-A, flip scored on disjoint cal-B) before sealing.
read_operator: GO-1 blind margin-sensitivity operator, top-r=32 low-rank subspace, fit on all calibration and applied unchanged to held-out

internal_split_cal_B.note: fit on cal-A, scored on DISJOINT cal-B -> generalises out-of-fit before sealing

verdict: CONFIRMED (4/4 sealed bars) on the held-out DISJOINT opinions. On the SAME held-out where GO-P-2026-035 failed (recon-optimal beat read-preserving, O=0.773 > R=0.757), the ONLY change -- the read operator, from estimated doc-covariance to the GO-1 blind margin-sensitivity -- flips it to read-preserving beating recon-optimal (R=0.779 > O=0.771, flip 200/200), while recon-optimal reconstructs the embeddings 2.6x better (O=0.220 vs R=0.584 -- the textbook dissociation). The consumer-relative flip DOES transfer to real legal-citation retrieval; the 035 miss was a read-operator-IDENTIFIABILITY failure, not a failure of the flip. This is also the first REAL-consumer demonstration that the GO-1 blind probe (previously only [predicted] on planted subspaces) recovers a read operator that is both misaligned-with-energy AND generalizing -- exactly the two conditions the D4/035 negatives showed are required.
